calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2023 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-27-2022 Set For Trial On Apr-03-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Order To Show Cause Re: Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-20-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Darren J.M. Orr; Legal Assistance to the Elderly; 415-538-3333; appearing in person on behalf of plaintiff Martin Levin.
Annette L. Rose; Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani LLP; 415-986-5900; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendant Board of Trustees of the California State University.

Plaintiff's counsel contests the court's tentative ruling. Mr. Orr states that defendant accepted rent from plaintiff, therefore establishing a lease agreement, and that the rent was accepted after defendant became aware that the original tenant had passed away. Mr. Orr further states that defendant has not demonstrated that they would be harmed by injunction, as they have no shortage of available housing, and plaintiff's possessions are being stored in a vacant apartment.

Ms. Rose argues that Mr. Orr's statements were not made in his moving papers, and that this dispute is being addressed in a separate forcible detainer action.

Mr. Orr states that plaintiff's supporting declaration does indeed state that rent was accepted after the death of the previous tenant.

After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: INJUNCTION is DENIED. Plaintiff has not demonstrated that he is likely to prevail on the merits or the likelihood of irreparable harm.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
